Tests division of a Boolean object by null resulting in positive infinity.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion statement that tests the behavior of JavaScript when dividing a `Boolean` object by `null`. The expression `new Boolean(true) / null` is expected to evaluate to `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY`, and the `assert` function checks if this condition is true. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of JavaScript's type coercion and arithmetic operations. It is likely used in a testing context to verify the correctness of JavaScript's handling of division involving `Boolean` objects and `null`.
